english: a monk, a king Dzl.; skra mťoṅ-mťiṅ-gi k'a-dóg-tu gyúr-to (skra mthong mthing gi kha dog tu 'gyur to) his hair turned azure (sky-blue) Dzl.; sbrúm-par _gyúr-ba (sbrum par 'gyur ba) to get with child; bdún-du _gyur-ba (bdun du 'gyur ba) to reach the number of seven Dzl. (In all these cases the more recent writings and the col. language in C. usually have *dó-wa*, in W. *čá-če*.) _gyúr-ba ('gyur ba) is also frq. used in conjunction with verbs: yód-par _gyúr-ba (yod par 'gyur ba) 'to become being', i.e. to begin to exist, 'to become having', i.e. to gain possession; sróg-la mi ltá-bar _gyúr-pai dṅós-po _di-dag (srog la mi lta bar 'gyur ba'i dngos po 'di dag) these acts of having become indifferent to life, i.e. acts of contempt of death Dzl.; ná-bar gyúr-na _di mťóṅ-ba tsám-gyis nad sós-par _gyúr-ro (na bar 'gyur na 'di mthong ba tsam gyis nad sos par 'gyur ro) when taken ill, they get well again, as soon as they obtain a sight of this Glr.; čaṅ mi smrá-bar _gyúr-to (cang mi smra bar 'gyur to) he became speechless Dzl.; _gyúr-ba ('gyur ba) denoting both the pass. voice, and the fut. tense, the context must decide in every instance, how it is to be understood: su žig rgyal-srid byéd-par _gyur (su zhig rgyal srid byed par 'gyur) who shall have the government, who shall rule? Tar. 21.; de rgyál-por _gyúr-bar šés-so (de rgyal por 'gyur bar shes so) they knew that this man is made king (for: would be made king); K'ó-mos k'yód-kyi bu bsád-par gyúr-na (kho mos khyod kyi bu bsad par 'gyur na) if your son has been killed by me Dzl.; k'yod mi-ša zá-bar gyúr-čig (khyod mi sha za bar 'gyur cig) may you be obliged to eat human flesh! Dzl.; čii p'yir k'yod _di-ltar gyur (ci'i phyir khyod 'di ltar gyur) by what means have you come into this state? Dzl.; ya-mtsán-du (ya mtshan du) (or -par) (-par) _gyúr-ba ('gyur ba) to be surprised, astonished; with γnás-su: (gnas su:) to come to a place, to arrive at Mil.; _dód-pai dṅos-grúb-tu _gyúr-ba ('dod pa'i dngos grub tu 'gyur ba) to be endowed with the perfect gift of wishing, viz. of having every wish fulfilled; to happen, to take place, to occur, ya-mtsán-du _gyúr-ba či yod (ya mtshan du 'gyur ba ci yod) lit. what is there that has wonderfully happened, what wonderful things have happened? γyós-par _gyúr-ba (g.yos par 'gyur ba) to become moving, to begin to move. — 3. to be translated, bod-du (bod du) into Tibetan Tar.; - bka-_gyúr (bka' 'gyur) the translated word, v. bka (bka'); cf. sgyúr-ba. (sgyur ba.) — 4. joined to numbers it signifies time or times, γžán-pas brgya-_gyúr stoṅ-_gyúr-du p'ágs-pa (gzhan pas brgya 'gyur stong 'gyur du 'phags pa) a hundred times, nay a thousand times more sublime than others Dzl.; k'yód-pas brgya-_gyúr-bas lhág-par bzáṅ-ba yod (khyod pas brgya 'gyur bas lhag par bzang ba yod) there are (girls) a hundred times prettier than you Dzl.; γsum-_gyúr ltá-bur (gsum 'gyur lta bur) three times as much Dzl.; de γnyis-_gyúr tsam žig (de gnyis 'gyur tsam zhig) one twice as large as that Mil. II. sbst. change, alteration, vicissitude, dus bžii _gyúr-bas (dus bzhi'i 'gyur bas) through the change of seasons Thgy. — _gyur-skád ('gyur skad), or also _gyur-k'úgs ('gyur khugs) singing or humming a tune in a trilling manner Mil.; _gyur-lčam nya ('gyur lcam nya) Mil. perh. a fish swiftly moving to and fro; _gyur-rtén bžág-pa ('gyur rten bzhag pa) to pay money in hand, as an earnest that the bargain is not to be retracted. — Instead of the imp. gyur ('gyur), šóg (shog) is frq. used.

pf. (and imp.?) gyes (gyes), to be divided, e.g. a river that is divided into several branches; rnám-pa γnyís-su (rnam pa gnyis su) (a ray of light divided) into two parts Dzl.; to separate, to part, bem rig gyes dus (bem rig gyes dus) when body and soul part from each other Mil.; to disperse, of a crowd, with or without so-sór (so sor) Dzl. and elsewh.; of a single person: to part, withdraw, go away, *mi-tsóg daṅ ghyē-nē* C. withdrawing from the crowd; to issue, proceed, spread, dé-dag-las gyés-so (de dag las gyes so) they have proceeded from those (their ancestors); of a disease: gyén-du gyes (gyen du gyes) (opp. to túr-du zug (thur du zug)) Med.?

(W. *kyé-če*) pf. bgyes (bgyes), fut. bkye (bkye), 1. to divide (trs.), to scatter, disperse, diffuse, e.g. rays of light; it is also used when the neutral form _gyé-ba ('gye ba) would seem to be more correct; to let proceed, sprúl-pa (sprul pa), an emanation; hence to send, a messenger Lex. and Schr.; to dismiss, tsogs (tshogs), an assembly Sch. — 2. _táb-mo _gyéd-pa ('thab mo 'gyed pa), γyul _gyéd-pa (g.yul 'gyed pa), also _gyéd-pa ('gyed pa) alone, to fight a battle, to fight, to combat, _gyéd-pai tse ('gyed pa'i tshe) in the dispute; similarly _dzin-ga bkyé-ba ('dzin ga bkye ba) to quarrel Med.; hence prob. W.: *k'a kyé-če* to abuse, to menace. — 3. stón-mo _gyéd-pa (ston mo 'gyed pa) frq. to give an entertainment, banquet, prop. to dispense a feast; nor _gyéd-pa (nor 'gyed pa) to distribute a property Lex.
to drop or let fall, to throw down Schr.; to quit, abandon, throw away Sch.
